description_long: This object is a standard cigarette, featuring a cylindrical shape with a white paper-wrapped body and a light brown filter at one end. The white section is smooth and uniform, representing the paper that encases the tobacco inside. The filter end is slightly darker and textured, indicating the fibrous material used to filter smoke. Both ends are flat, with the filter end appearing solid and the opposite end showing a faintly darkened, open area where tobacco would be visible. There are no visible brand markings, logos, or decorative elements on the exterior. The cigarette appears unused, with no signs of burning, ash, or charring.
description: A cylindrical cigarette with a white paper body and a light brown filter, showing no burn marks or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cigarette, with the white paper body and brown filter clearly visible.
description_view_1: End-on view of the cigarette, displaying the circular cross-section of the filter and a hint of the white paper wrapping.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the white paper body and brown filter reversed in orientation.
description_view_3: End-on view from the tobacco side, showing the circular cross-section of the white paper and a faintly darkened tip.
